assess.title: Analyze Unit 3 Assessment Results
assess.desc: Unit 3 end-of-unit assessment was given Friday. Analyze results to identify gaps in slope, y = mx + b, and calculating slope from two points before diving into Unit 4.

revise.title: Adjust Remaining Lessons (Coteach)
revise.desc: Use coteach.ai to adjust Unit 4 Lessons 3–4 based on how Lessons 1–2 went and what the assessment data showed. Add scaffolding where students struggled.

oko-slope.title: OKO Small Group: Calculating Slope
oko-slope.desc: Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos all struggled with calculating slope from two points on the Unit 3 assessment. Set them up with OKO to practice finding slope and interpreting rate of change in real-world contexts from Lessons 7 and 10.

marcus.title: Extra Practice: Marcus
marcus.desc: Marcus confuses slope and y-intercept, so he needs support from Unit 3 Lessons 5–8. Assign him 1-on-1 practice on writing equations in y = mx + b form from a graph.

aaliyah.title: Extra Practice: Aaliyah
aaliyah.desc: Aaliyah has a gap from Grade 7 Unit 2 on proportional relationships (y = kx) that's blocking her understanding of linear vs. proportional. Assign her 1-on-1 practice identifying the constant of proportionality.
aaliyah.math: y = kx: if 3 pizzas cost $24, find k

oko-equations.title: OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables
oko-equations.desc: Maya, DeShawn, and Imani can read tables but aren't connecting rate of change to slope yet. Set them up with OKO to practice writing y = mx + b from tables, building on Unit 3 Lessons 5–7.

challenge.title: Challenge: Equation Error Analysis
challenge.desc: Darius and Zara aced the Unit 3 assessment and are ready to move ahead. Give them Unit 4 Lesson 4 cool-down style problems where they critique solution paths and find errors in multi-step equations.

analyze.title: Analyze Practice for Common Bugs
analyze.desc: Review Unit 3 cool-downs and practice problems to find patterns in student errors around slope, y-intercept, and writing equations of lines
analyze.math: Bug: reads y-int as slope in y = 3 + 2x

video.title: Create Video: Slope from Two Points
video.desc: Record explainer video students watch at home on calculating slope from two points, using rise/run and the formula — the most common Unit 3 gap

game.title: Build Hanger Diagram Game
game.desc: Create an interactive game where students balance hanger diagrams and match them to equations — supports Unit 4 Lessons 2–3 conceptual foundation

assign-game.title: Assign Game to Class
assign-game.desc: Push the hanger diagram game to all students as extra practice after Unit 4 Lesson 3 Balanced Moves

timeline.text: ── Monday, Nov 3 ──
Morning! I gave the Unit 3 assessment on Friday. Can you analyze the results while I teach Lesson 1 today? I'm jumping straight into Unit 4.
On it. I'll have the results analyzed by this afternoon. In the meantime, here are the tasks I'd recommend for this week alongside your Lessons 1–4. Please review and approve.
✓ 11 tasks created · 4 with dependencies
Looks good. Let me go through them.
Hold off on the extension for Darius and Zara. Let me see the assessment results first.
Got it — challenge card on hold until we have data.
✓ 10 approved · 1 on hold
Starting the assessment analysis and the OKO equations group. Maya, DeShawn, and Imani will work with OKO while you teach Lesson 1.
→ Assessment Analysis & OKO Equations → In Progress
Just finished Lesson 1 — Writing Equivalent Equations. Most kids got the idea that 2x + 3 = 7 and 2x = 4 are equivalent, but about a third couldn't explain WHY. They just want to memorize steps.
That's common with Lesson 1. For tomorrow's Lesson 2 on hanger diagrams, try having students explain the 'why' using the balance metaphor before writing any symbols. That bridges the conceptual gap.
OKO session complete! Maya nailed rate of change from tables (92%). DeShawn (85%) still confuses the initial value with the first y-value. Imani (88%) is solid.
✓ OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables → Done
── Tuesday, Nov 4 ──
Assessment results are in:

• 58% struggled to calculate slope from two coordinate points
• 6 students can't reliably write y = mx + b from a graph
• Aaliyah, Jayden, and 3 others still shaky on proportional relationships (Grade 7 gap)
• Darius and Zara aced everything — ready for extension
→ Assessment Analysis → Review
Those results confirm what I saw in Lesson 1. Go ahead and start Marcus on Brisk and the slope group on OKO. And since Darius and Zara clearly don't need review — approve the challenge card.
Approved! Starting interventions. Marcus gets Brisk on slope vs. y-intercept, OKO runs the slope group with Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos.
→ OKO Slope & Marcus → In Progress

Just taught Lesson 2 — Keeping the Equation Balanced. The hanger diagrams were a hit! But Jayden and a few others kept wanting to 'solve' without explaining the balance. Marcus was really lost — glad he's getting Brisk time.
Good to hear the hangers worked. For Jayden — OKO is specifically working on the conceptual 'why' of each move, which should help. Also, Marcus just had a breakthrough on Brisk.

Marcus completed his Brisk session — he was reading the coefficient of x as the y-intercept. After working through three graphs where he plotted the y-intercept first, then used slope to find the next point, he got 11/12 correct.
That's great! I noticed the same thing in class — he kept mixing up m and b. I'll check in with him tomorrow to see if it sticks.
✓ Extra Practice: Marcus → Done
── Wednesday, Nov 5 ──
OKO slope group wrapped up. Aisha and Carlos can now calculate slope from two points and explain it in context. Jayden still reverses rise and run sometimes — flagging for follow-up.
✓ OKO Slope group → Done
Starting Aaliyah's Brisk practice on proportional relationships. Also — assessment analysis is approved, so I'm unlocking the lesson revision task with coteach.ai.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Assessment → Lesson Revision

Just finished Lesson 3 — Balanced Moves. This was TOUGH. When we moved from hanger diagrams to actual equations, about half the class couldn't connect the moves. Can coteach.ai add more bridge problems for Lesson 4 tomorrow?
Absolutely. I'll have coteach.ai add transition problems to Lesson 4 that show the hanger diagram side-by-side with the equation for each step. Students can see both representations at once.
Aaliyah completed her Brisk practice. She can now distinguish y = kx (proportional, through origin) from y = mx + b (linear, with y-intercept). That Grade 7 gap is closed.
✓ Aaliyah → Done · Challenge group started
── Thursday, Nov 6 ──
coteach.ai finished revising Lesson 4 with the hanger-to-equation bridge problems you asked for. Also starting the practice bug analysis and the hanger diagram game today.
→ Lesson revision → Review · Analysis & Game → In Progress
Lesson 4 went WAY better than yesterday! The side-by-side hanger/equation problems from coteach.ai made a huge difference. Even DeShawn was explaining the balanced moves to his partner. 🙌
That's great to hear! I'll note that the side-by-side scaffold worked so we can use it again. Meanwhile, the practice analysis found the top 3 bugs across the class:

1. Confusing slope and y-intercept when equation is y = b + mx (41%)
2. Calculating slope as run/rise instead of rise/run (28%)
3. Not recognizing that a negative slope means the line goes down (19%)

Creating a video on slope from two points.
That y = b + mx confusion is real — I see it every year. Make sure the video shows both forms side by side so kids recognize the slope no matter where it appears.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Analysis → Video creation
── Friday, Nov 7 ──

Last day of the week! Video is rendered with both equation forms side by side as you asked. Hanger diagram game is ready. And Darius and Zara crushed the error analysis — they found all 4 errors in Lin's solution path.
→ Video & Game → Review · Challenge → Done
The revised Lesson 4 materials look great — approve them. Push the slope video for weekend homework and assign the hanger game as extra practice.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Game → Assign to class

Week complete! 🎉

📚 Lessons 1–4 taught — hanger diagrams clicked after coteach.ai added bridge problems
📊 Unit 3 gaps identified — slope from two points was the biggest
👥 Both OKO groups closed key gaps
👤 Marcus and Aaliyah caught up via Brisk
🌟 Darius & Zara completed error analysis extension
🎬 Slope video assigned for weekend
🎮 Hanger diagram game assigned for extra practice
Great week. Lessons 1–4 are done and I feel way better about where the kids are. The coteach.ai adjustment for Lesson 4 was a game-changer. See you Monday for Lesson 5! 🙏
✓ All tasks complete · Lessons 1–4 taught · On to Lesson 5 Monday!
